methods.description: Collection of samples from various Ephedra species distributed in the QTP and adjacent regions for phylogeographical study based on cpDNA sequences.

steps.description: Young branchlets were collected from 107 populations (totaling 1435 individuals), most of which were represented by individuals that were at least 50 m apart from each other.
steps.searchable_summary: collect young branchlets from 107 populations of Ephedra species

steps.description: One individual of the Mediterranean E. nebrodensis was sampled as outgroup based on previous phylogenetic analyses.
37 species were sampled in the combined cpDNA analysis.

methods.method_name: DNA Sequence Alignment and Diversity Analysis
methods.description: Alignment of DNA sequences and estimation of molecular diversity indices for each population and species.

steps.description: The DNA sequences were aligned using the program Clustal X and manually adjusted in BioEdit v. 7.0.9.
steps.searchable_summary: align DNA sequences using Clustal X and adjust in BioEdit

steps.description: Arlequin 3.11 was used to estimate the molecular diversity indices, including the number of segregating sites (S), number of haplotypes (N h ), haplotype diversity (H d ) and nucleotide diversity (p), for each population and species.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate molecular diversity indices using Arlequin 3.11

methods.description: Reconstruction of evolutionary relationships of chlorotypes using maximum parsimony, maximum likelihood, and Bayesian inference.

steps.description: The evolutionary relationships of the chlorotypes were reconstructed with maximum parsimony (MP), maximum likelihood (ML) and Bayesian inference (BI), using PAUP*4.0b10, PhyML 3.0, and MrBayes 3.1.2, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct evolutionary relationships using MP, ML, and BI

steps.description: Bootstrap analysis was conducted with 1000 replicates using the same heuristic search settings.

methods.description: Estimation of divergence times of chlorotypes using molecular clock analysis and BEAST software.

steps.description: Divergence times were estimated with BEAST v1.7.2, using the HKY+I+G substitution model, an uncorrelated lognormal relaxed clock model, the Yule model of speciation and a user-specified starting tree (ML tree).
steps.searchable_summary: estimate divergence times using BEAST with HKY+I+G model

steps.description: The MCMC analysis was run for 10,000,000 generations to estimate the mean posterior divergence times with standard deviations.
